title: CVE-2024-1753 container escape fix
body:
This addresses CVE-2024-1753, which allows a user to write files to the `/` directory of the host machine if SELinux was not enabled.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read and understood our contributing guidelines

that all your commits are signed with `git commit -s`.
-->

If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note

comments.body:
@mheon @nalind @edsantiago PTAL.  If this LGTY, I'll start cherry-picking.

@cebarks FYI
LGTM
@nalind PTAL and merge
Probably best to have someone other than me reviewing a patch I wrote.
@flouthoc @Luap99 PTAL
@nalind if you could review to make sure I didn't mess it up, but not as an official "L_G_T_M", that would be appreciated.
Can confirm this includes the fix I suggested.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99*, *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

For the record, LGTM for that instance of the code. I did confirm that this seems to be the only place relevant to `type="bind"` mounts; I didn’t audit the codebase as a whole for the category of issues.
/lgtm
@Luap99, good point on the extra run.  I will merge this as is (assuming happy tests), but I will circle back to change that to a comment.  I'd put that into my testing as it was a handy marker
/unhold
/hold cancel
New changes are detected. LGTM label has been removed.
@rhatdan please stop merging code that didn’t pass tests. We are never in _that much_ of a hurry.
@nalind @TomSweeneyRedHat I think we'll need this backported into release-1.33 so that it could be vendored into podman v4.9 branch? WDYT?

/cherrypick release-1.33

I'm going ahead with cherrypick. You can close it later if you don't feel the need.

@lsm5: #5411 failed to apply on top of branch "release-1.33":
```

Auto-merging internal/volumes/volumes.go
error: Failed to merge in the changes.
hint: Use 'git am --show-current-patch=diff' to see the failed patch

When you have resolved this problem, run "git am --continue".
If you prefer to skip this patch, run "git am --skip" instead.
```

>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.

Podman v4.9 branch SGTM. We'll need to do both 4.9 and 4.9-rhel but the patches should be identical.
release-1.33 backport at https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5421
@lsm5 as I think you discovered, I have a cherry-pick in flight for this and a couple of other versions.  Working on backporting in multiple releases.
